message:
Ever wonder how your dog "reads" the whole neighborhood on a single walk? That nose is basically a superpower. 🐾

Dogs have up to 300 million scent receptors — humans have around 6 million. They can even breathe in and out at the same time while sniffing, keeping a constant stream of information flowing. Experts say a dog could pick out a single teaspoon of sugar dissolved in a million gallons of water.

That slow, sniffy walk where they stop at every blade of grass? That's your dog catching up on the news. Let them sniff — it's how they see the world. 🐶❤️
